summary: Joel Spolsky announces that Eric Sink will host a new Business of Software forum on Joel's discussion site. The brief post serves primarily as an introduction to Eric Sink's credentials, highlighting his extensive career including building the Spyglass web browser, founding the AbiWord project, and running SourceGear. Joel positions Sink as uniquely qualified due to his deep industry experience and generous knowledge-sharing about the software business.
key_points: Eric Sink is taking on hosting duties for a new Business of Software discussion forum
Sink's credentials span decades: early web browsers (Spyglass), open source (AbiWord), and a successful Inc 500 company (SourceGear)
Joel frames Sink simultaneously as friend, teacher, and competitor, suggesting mutual respect in the small software business community
money_quotes: I'm happy to announce that my friend, teacher, and competitor Eric Sink has agreed to host the new Business of Software forum.
He has been extremely generous with his knowledge of software business.
key_insight: This is a brief announcement post introducing Eric Sink as host of a new business-focused forum, notable mainly for how Joel highlights the collegial relationships among independent software entrepreneurs of the era.
